codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly hardens fail-closed behavior for auth/preflight paths, async terminalization bounds, and token redaction. All four VAL assertions (002, 004, 009, 010) are well-covered with existing code and tests. The handoff indicates no new files were added — the worker verified existing implementations were complete and all tests pass (44 webhook tests + 15 cross-area tests + full lint/typecheck).

issues.description: The async safety terminalization logic (setTimeout + force-terminalization at 5 minutes) has no dedicated unit test. The behavior is exercised indirectly via cross-area flow tests, but there is no test that specifically verifies: (a) a run that never resolves gets force-terminalized, (b) the 'force_terminalized' log event fires, (c) the status becomes 'partial' with terminal=true after the timeout. The cross-area tests cover normal async completion paths but not the stall/timeout safety path. This is non-blocking because the code logic is straightforward and the 5-minute bound is clearly documented.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The webhook handler follows a strict ordering convention: method check → auth check → parse request → extract/redact googleAccessToken → preflight → status store write → run execution. This ordering is critical for security (auth before any side effects) but is not documented in AGENTS.md. Workers touching the webhook handler need to know this ordering invariant.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handle-discovery-webhook.ts lines 82-185 show the sequential ordering. The preflight and auth checks both return early before any runStatusStore.put() calls.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The webhook handler uses timingSafeEqual from node:crypto for secret comparison, and separately checks buffer lengths before calling timingSafeEqual. This is a deliberate defense-in-depth pattern to prevent timing attacks. This pattern could be useful for future workers implementing similar auth checks.

summary: PASS — The implementation fully satisfies all four VAL assertions (002, 004, 009, 010). Auth fails closed using timing-safe comparison with explicit categorized error responses. Preflight readiness checks block invalid runs before any status/lineage is created. Async runs have bounded terminalization via a 5-minute safety timeout. Token material (googleAccessToken) is stripped from the request object before passing to runDiscovery and kept out of persisted run-status payloads. Test coverage is strong with dedicated tests for auth rejection (401 on missing/mismatched/empty secret), token stripping, token non-persistence, preflight blocking (credential readiness, blank intent), and async lineage consistency. One non-blocking observation: the safety terminalization code path lacks a dedicated unit test for the stall scenario.
